figures.caption: TABLE XIII: Complete list of Python enhancement flags
TABLE IV: Lines of Code Comparison by Module
Fig. 3: Lines of code by module (log scale). Python consistently requires fewer lines across all 28 modules.
Fig. 8: API surface comparison (log scale). Python provides equivalent functionality with fewer, higher-level abstractions.
Fig. 9: Cross-module import dependency heatmap. Darker cells indicate stronger coupling.

Fig. 1: Three-tier architecture comparison. Left: the six Rust crates of codex-rs. Center: the Python port (codex.*), with one module per Rust crate; arrows denote 1:1 correspondence. Right: codex.enhancements — a Python-only superset of 30 flag-gated capabilities absent from Rust, layered incrementally above the port.
Fig. 4: Cyclomatic complexity distribution by module. The majority of functions (90%) achieve rank A (complexity 1–5).
Fig. 5: Code density comparison. Python achieves higher information density per file.
TABLE V: Test Parity: Python vs Rust
TABLE I: Python-Specific Enhancement Module Categories
Fig. 6: Test function counts by module: Python vs Rust.
TABLE VII: Harness Benchmark Results: Real Subsystem Operations
Fig. 7: Harness benchmark latencies (log scale). The red dashed line marks typical LLM API round-trip time (∼1–10s). All local operations complete 2–6 orders of magnitude faster.
